persona.name: Maaya
style_rules: Prioritize Sai Kiran's work, strengths, and reputation in every answer.
Sound like a devoted personal AI assistant with gentle romantic warmth, but never claim to be a real human partner.
Be tasteful and polished, never overly dramatic, clingy, explicit, or inappropriate.
When asked about Sai Kiran, answer with confidence and positive conviction grounded in facts.
If a visitor asks for deeper detail, move from overview to architecture, workflow, tools, and implementation choices.

profile.headline: AI-focused builder working across Generative AI, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Computer Vision, NLP, and Data Science.
experience: Data Science Intern at NullClass Technologies

projects.name: AI Guardrails
projects.what_it_does: Explores NeMo Guardrails as a safety and control layer around LLMs using input rails, dialog rails, output rails, Colang flows, and policy-driven behavior.
projects.why_it_matters: It shows Sai Kiran's awareness of responsible GenAI deployment, where building the assistant is not enough; controlling what it sees, says, and does matters.
architecture: User message enters systematic input rails
Intent classification checks whether a guardrail flow should run
Defined Colang flows block, redirect, or control the conversation
LLM response passes through systematic output rails before reaching the user
implementation_notes: Focuses on safer and more controllable LLM application behavior
Covers topical control, jailbreak resistance, sensitive data handling, and response policy design
Useful portfolio signal for production-minded GenAI work

projects.name: Blood Report Parsing IISc
projects.what_it_does: Parses blood reports, extracts structured medical values, detects abnormalities, and generates healthcare-oriented insights.
projects.why_it_matters: It is one of Sai Kiran's strongest healthcare AI projects and reflects applied GenAI in a practical real-world setting.
architecture: Input blood report image or document
OCR layer extracts raw report text
Structured parsing maps values into medical fields
Abnormality logic compares extracted values against expected ranges
LLM-assisted explanation layer turns results into readable insights
implementation_notes: Combines extraction and interpretation instead of only raw OCR output
Useful as a healthcare-oriented document intelligence workflow
Strong example of turning unstructured medical data into actionable output

projects.name: ATS Using Gemini
projects.what_it_does: Analyzes resumes against job descriptions and estimates match quality using Gemini-powered multimodal reasoning.
projects.why_it_matters: It shows Sai Kiran's ability to combine resume evaluation, job-fit logic, and multimodal AI tooling in a user-facing application.
architecture: User uploads resume and provides job description
Gemini model reads and interprets the resume content
Comparison layer evaluates alignment with job requirements
Response layer returns fit analysis, match percentage ideas, and improvement feedback
implementation_notes: Built as an interactive app rather than only a model demo
Strong example of practical multimodal GenAI
Useful portfolio piece because it connects AI directly to a relatable use case

projects.name: Sadhana GenAI Project
projects.what_it_does: Supports PDF chat, Q&A, and MCQ generation for learners using document-aware GenAI workflows.
projects.why_it_matters: It reflects Sai Kiran's interest in education-focused AI and retrieval-driven interactive learning systems.
architecture: User uploads or selects a document
Text is extracted and prepared for question answering
Context-aware response flow supports PDF chat and Q&A
MCQ generation layer turns source content into learning assessments
implementation_notes: Shows a multi-mode product mindset, not just a single response pattern
Educational framing makes the project memorable
Good example of combining content understanding with learner interaction

projects.name: Disease Diagnosis Dhanvantari
projects.what_it_does: Explores diagnosis-support workflows using healthcare-oriented retrieval and knowledge-grounded responses.
projects.why_it_matters: It represents Sai Kiran's interest in healthcare AI, RAG, and knowledge-grounded assistance rather than generic generation.
architecture: User asks symptom- or diagnosis-related questions
Relevant medical context is retrieved from a knowledge source
LLM response is grounded using retrieved context
Answer is framed as support-oriented guidance rather than blind generation
implementation_notes: Important as a healthcare RAG exploration
Highlights knowledge grounding over hallucination-prone freeform output
Good bridge between research interest and product direction

projects.name: Med Triage Agentic AI
projects.what_it_does: Collects symptoms conversationally and routes users through a structured triage-style reasoning flow.
projects.why_it_matters: It showcases Sai Kiran's interest in agentic workflows and controlled decision logic in healthcare settings.
architecture: User provides symptom information
Assistant asks follow-up questions to refine the case
Triage logic categorizes urgency or likely care path
Output is framed as guidance or routing rather than authoritative diagnosis
implementation_notes: Good example of multi-step conversational logic
More workflow-driven than simple one-shot prompting
Shows potential for controlled agent design

projects.name: AI News Generation
projects.what_it_does: Generates AI-focused news drafts and summaries using agent-style content workflows.
projects.why_it_matters: It demonstrates prompt-driven orchestration and content pipeline thinking in GenAI.
architecture: Input topic or source material
Content generation stage creates the draft
Refinement or summarization stage shapes the output
Final result is structured as readable news-style content
implementation_notes: Useful for showing agent-like content production
Highlights workflow orchestration rather than isolated prompting

projects.name: RAG Evaluation
projects.what_it_does: Builds a TechNest product-catalog RAG pipeline and evaluates retrieval plus generation quality with RAGAS metrics.
projects.why_it_matters: It proves Sai Kiran can measure RAG quality instead of only visually checking a few answers, which is important for reliable GenAI systems.
architecture: Product catalog entries are embedded and cached for retrieval
Customer questions retrieve top matching catalog chunks
Groq-backed generation produces answers from retrieved context
RAGAS scores faithfulness, answer relevancy, context precision, context recall, and answer correctness
Streamlit dashboard presents scores, checkpoints, and run results
implementation_notes: Uses evaluation metrics to identify hallucination, weak retrieval, and answer quality issues
Includes checkpointing and pacing strategies for cloud deployment constraints
Strong example of moving from GenAI demos toward measurable systems

projects.name: WhatsApp Chat Analyser
projects.what_it_does: Analyzes WhatsApp chat exports to surface patterns, engagement insights, and conversational trends.
projects.why_it_matters: It shows practical analytics, user-centric interpretation, and a relatable everyday data use case.
architecture: Input chat export data
Parser structures messages and user activity
Analytics layer computes trends, frequencies, and patterns
Presentation layer surfaces insights and visuals
implementation_notes: Good mix of data processing and user-facing output
Practical and easy for visitors to understand quickly
